hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz C204 Coupé Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the C204 C-Class Coupé — from the 1.6-litre turbo to the 6.2-litre V8. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the OM651 diesel that transformed the C-Class running-cost equation. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes-Benz C204 Coupé Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the C204 coupé specifically — petrol, diesel and AMG V8 power across the 2011–2015 production run.

overview.intro: The Mercedes-Benz C204 is the two-door coupé variant of the third-generation C-Class (W204), unveiled in 2011 alongside the mid-cycle facelift of the wider 204 range. It marked the return of a true compact two-door model to the Mercedes lineup, replacing the CLC (CL203) as a purpose-built coupé engineered alongside the facelifted W204 saloon. Nearly every body panel from the A-pillar rearward was unique to the C204, with a long hood, short rear deck and frameless doors defining its classic coupé silhouette. Engine choice ranged from the 1.6-litre M274 turbo four to the 6.2-litre M156 V8 in the C 63 AMG, with the OM651 2.1-litre diesel providing the most compelling running-cost story in the range.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes-Benz W204 (C204 coupé variant) • Predecessor: CLC-Class (CL203) • Successor: C205 Coupé • Sibling body styles in this generation: W204 Saloon, S204 Estate.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The C204 coupé attracts strong specialist interest in the UK used market, with enquiries concentrated on OM651 diesel pre-purchase inspections and M271 timing chain concerns [PATTERN DATA].

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode applied — compressed to a single paragraph. Best & Worst Engines: The C204's engine range splits into three distinct reliability tiers. At the top sits the OM651 2.1-litre diesel (C 220 CDI/C 250 CDI) — widely regarded as Mercedes' most durable modern four-cylinder diesel, with fewer timing chain concerns than its OM646 predecessor and strong fuel economy. The M274 1.6-litre turbo petrol (C 180, 2012-on) is the pick of the petrol fours — a more modern design than the M271, with better reliability and lower running costs. The M271 1.8-litre turbo family (C 180 CGI/C 200 CGI/C 250 CGI) sits in the middle — strong performers but documented timing chain and turbocharger issues as mileage climbs. The M276 3.5-litre V6 (C 350) offers smooth refinement and genuine performance, but its complexity makes it the most expensive to maintain outside warranty. For most buyers, the OM651 diesel represents the best ownership proposition — strong torque, excellent economy and proven durability. Who it's for: buyers who want the sweet spot between performance and running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Solid Choice — more reliable than M271
Watch — injectors are the weak point

economicsRule.title: The C204 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£4,800 OM651 recon against a £6,000–£10,000 C204 sits at 28–80% of the car's value. On a sub-£6,000 example, that brushes our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £10,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 28–48% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C204 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM651 diesel — strong fuel economy, proven durability.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C204 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M276 V6 — complex, expensive parts, specialist labour required.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C204 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: C 220 CDI (OM651) — the sweet spot between economy and performance.

problems.title: M271 Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The M271's timing chain tensioner can fail, allowing the chain to stretch or skip — a well-documented issue across the Mercedes four-cylinder turbo family. Early warning signs include a rattle on cold start and reduced performance.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the timing chain inspected immediately — an £800–£1,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £2,500–£4,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare C204 M271 timing chain repair quotes →

problems.title: OM651 Injector Seal Failure (Black Death)
problems.rootCause: Injector seals can fail, allowing combustion gases to leak past and create carbon buildup around the injector — known in the trade as "Black Death". If left unaddressed, the injector can seize in the cylinder head.
problems.recommendation: Have injector seals inspected at every service — a £400–£800 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £1,800–£3,000 cylinder head repair that follows a seized injector.

cta.label: Compare C204 OM651 injector repair quotes →

problems.title: M276 Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The M276 V6 can suffer from timing chain stretch, particularly on earlier production cars. The chain tensioner and guides wear over time, leading to rattle on start-up and potential chain skip.
problems.recommendation: If your C 350 exhibits cold-start rattle, have the timing chain inspected immediately — £1,200–£2,000 now is far cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows a failure.

cta.label: Compare C204 M276 timing chain repair quotes →

problems.title: M274 Water Pump Failure
problems.rootCause: The M274's water pump and thermostat housing can develop leaks, leading to coolant loss and potential overheating if not addressed promptly.
problems.recommendation: Monitor coolant levels regularly — a £400–£800 water pump replacement is cheap insurance against a £1,800+ engine replacement.

cta.label: Compare C204 M274 water pump repair quotes →

replacementCosts.h2: C204 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

items.question: How much does a Mercedes-Benz C204 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned C204 engine typically costs between £1,800 and £5,500 supply only [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet], depending on engine — the volume OM651 diesel sits at £2,800–£4,800. Fitting adds 8–16 hours of labour at specialist rates — budget an additional £520–£1,040 for installation. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which Mercedes-Benz C204 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The OM651 2.1-litre diesel (C 220 CDI/C 250 CDI) is widely regarded as the most durable C204 engine — fewer timing chain concerns than its OM646 predecessor, strong fuel economy and proven longevity beyond 150,000 miles. The M274 1.6-litre turbo petrol (C 180, 2012-on) is the pick of the petrol fours.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz C204?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch on the M271 turbo petrols (C 180 CGI/C 200 CGI/C 250 CGI), injector seal failure ("Black Death") on the OM651 diesel, timing chain stretch on the M276 V6 (C 350), and water pump failure on the M274 (C 180) are the most documented issues. Each requires different preventative strategies.

items.question: Is the Mercedes-Benz C204 reliable?
items.answer: It depends heavily on which engine you choose. The OM651 diesel and M274 petrol four are generally reliable with routine maintenance. The M271 family requires careful chain history checking. The M276 V6 is smooth but complex — budget for specialist maintenance. Overall, a well-maintained C204 is a solid used proposition.

items.question: Which Mercedes-Benz C204 engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine outright — but a pre-2012 M271 without documented timing chain replacement is a purchase we consistently advise against. A neglected OM651 without injector seal history is the second. The M276 V6 is a premium choice; avoid it if you're not prepared for specialist running costs.

items.question: How do I know if my C204 engine needs replacing?
items.answer: Cold-start rattle (M271/M276 timing chain), rough idle or loss of power (OM651 injector seals), coolant loss (M274 water pump), or blue smoke from the exhaust are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a C204 engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £10,000 C204, a £4,000 OM651 recon is 40% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £5,000 example, the same recon is 80% — walk away. Our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold is the line.

items.question: How long do Mercedes-Benz C204 engines last?
items.answer: The OM651 diesel can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The M274 petrol four is similarly durable. The M271 and M276's limiting factor is the timing chain — addressed proactively, either can comfortably reach 120,000+ miles.

items.question: What is the difference between the C204, W204 and S204?
items.answer: All three share the third-generation C-Class platform and most engines. The C204 is the 2-door coupé (2011–2015, this page), the W204 is the 4-door saloon (2007–2014), and the S204 is the 5-door estate (2007–2014). Costs vary slightly by body style due to labour access — see each generation's own page for specifics.

items.question: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz C204 engine code?
items.answer: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on M271/M274/OM651/OM646/M276 units. Tell us your variant and approximate registration date and we'll identify it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will confirm it for free.
